prose: Fukui reactivity indices computed for ferrocene (Cp₂Fe, 21 atoms) using the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method (Bannwarth et al., J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2019, 15, 1652–1671) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Cation (N−1): charge +1, multiplicity 2; anion (N+1): charge −1, multiplicity 2. Charges partitioned via Mulliken scheme. Geometry: user-supplied ferrocene.xyz, no re-optimization. All integrity checks passed (sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000). No warnings reported. The iron center (atom 11) is the most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 0.22885456) and the most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.25938191), with a dual descriptor Δf = −0.030527 (negative → nucleophilic character). All 10 Cp carbon atoms and both Cp rings are symmetry-equivalent, each C carrying f⁺ ≈ 0.01364 and f⁻ ≈ 0.01218.
